summary: DHH recaps the fourth 'Building of Basecamp' workshop held in Seattle, noting improvements in delivery thanks to familiarity with the material and Apple's Keynote 2 presenter tools. The audience pushed back on their views about functional specs and contract-driven development, leading to better discussion. He mentions plans for future workshops every two-three months, possibly in New York next. The post also covers his Seattle itinerary including visiting Ruby community figures and picking up newly released Apple products.
key_points: Repeated delivery of the same workshop material led to more confident, polished presentations
Apple's Keynote 2 presenter view with slide previews and timers meaningfully improved presentation flow
Audience pushback on functional specs and contract-driven development created more valuable discussion
The early Rails community was tight-knit, with figures like Dave Thomas of Pragmatic Programmers attending workshops
money_quotes: We didn't make as many radical changes to the presentation this time as we've done before, so the material was more intimate to us and we are able to deliver it in a more convincing and timely manner.
We also had significantly more push-back from the audience on our views on functional specs and contract-driven development, which gave a more varied discussion.
key_insight: Iterating on workshop presentations and welcoming audience pushback leads to sharper, more engaging delivery of opinionated development practices.
